---
title: "Figure S2 (supplement to Figure 2) — recovery cloud colour-coded by per-recording maximized logL"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
EXPLORATORY alternative to Figure 2 Part A. Same recovery clouds (per-recording MLEs) for the two
parameter pairs across the 5 approximations, but each point is COLOUR-CODED by the maximized
log-likelihood of that recording (logL at its own MLE). The question this figure asks: do the
outlier / mis-recovered estimates sit at low logL, i.e. does logL flag the bad recoveries?

DATA: the same {base}_mle_cloud_runs.csv files read by figure_2.Rmd (../data/433ed13, skip=1).
Within one battery cell (Num_ch = 100, interval = 0.1 tau, group_size = 10) a recording is keyed by
sample_index (1000 recordings per algorithm, sample_index 0..999). Two kinds of rows are used:
  - component_path == "Model_Parameters_Hat": one value per parameter per recording (the cloud
    coordinates), param_name in {on, off, unitary_current, Num_ch_mean}.
  - component_path == "logL": ONE maximized logL per recording (param columns empty).
The join is on (algorithm, sample_index) inside the fixed cell -> one logL per cloud point.

TWO views of the same data:
  PANEL (global scale): raw logL on a single shared viridis colourbar (robust global clip). The
    dominant, real signal is the BETWEEN-algorithm logL gap (naive NR/MNR ~ -2200, recursive
    R/MR/IR ~ -1450). Within any one cloud the colour is nearly uniform.
  PANEL (per-algorithm scale): logL rescaled to each algorithm's own robust range so within-cloud
    variation is visible. This is the panel that actually answers the exploratory question.

FINDING (annotated on the per-algorithm panel): the Spearman correlation between a recording's logL
and its Mahalanobis outlier distance from the cloud centre is ~0 in every cell (rho in -0.05..0.05),
so the low-logL points are NOT the mis-recovered / outlier ones -> logL does not flag bad recoveries.
The kinetic row (on, off) is therefore just colour speckle. The ONE real structure logL carries is a
gradient ALONG the N_ch/i degeneracy ridge: rho(logL, N_ch) ~ +0.4..+0.7, rho(logL, i) ~ -0.7..-0.85
(higher N_ch / lower i -> higher logL). That is why the amplitude row shows a smooth along-ridge
colour gradient, yet the outlier-distance rho stays ~0: the gradient runs along the ellipse's long
axis, which Mahalanobis distance normalises away. So the slope is over the sloppy (identifiable-only-
as-a-product) direction, not over recovery quality. Net: a negative-result diagnostic, not a main
figure.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)   # squish (out-of-bounds clamping for the clipped colourbar)
```

```{r params}
DATA_DIR  <- "../data/433ed13"
REP_NCH      <- 100
REP_NSIM     <- 10000
REP_NOISE    <- "0.1"
REP_GROUP    <- 10
REP_INTERVAL <- 0.1

ALGOS    <- c("macro_NR","macro_NMR","macro_R","macro_MR","macro_IR")
ALGO_LAB <- c(macro_NR="NR", macro_NMR="MNR", macro_R="R", macro_MR="MR", macro_IR="IR")

PARAM_IDX <- c(on = 0, off = 1, unitary_current = 2, Num_ch_mean = 5)
# plotmath-parseable labels (rendered as proper subscripted math on axes and strips)
PARAM_LAB <- c(on = "log[10]~k[on]", off = "log[10]~k[off]",
               unitary_current = "log[10]~i", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")
TRUTH <- c(on = log10(10), off = log10(100),
           unitary_current = log10(1), Num_ch_mean = log10(REP_NCH))

near_int <- function(x) abs(x - REP_INTERVAL) < 1e-9
```

```{r read}
# read_skip1: same skip=1 provenance-row convention as figure_2.Rmd; fread + column selection keeps
# the five ~70 MB files light (only the columns this figure needs).
KEEP <- c("sample_index","component_path","statistic","param_name","algorithm",
          "Num_ch","interval_in_tau","group_size","value")
read_skip1 <- function(path) fread(path, skip = 1, select = KEEP, showProgress = FALSE)
base  <- file.path(DATA_DIR,
  sprintf("figure_3_nch_%d_nsim_%d_%s_noise_%s", REP_NCH, REP_NSIM, ALGOS, REP_NOISE))
cloud <- rbindlist(lapply(paste0(base, "_mle_cloud_runs.csv"), read_skip1), fill = TRUE)

# cloud coordinates: one value per parameter per recording, keyed by (algorithm, sample_index)
pts <- cloud %>% as_tibble() %>%
  filter(component_path == "Model_Parameters_Hat", statistic == "value",
         Num_ch == REP_NCH, group_size == REP_GROUP, near_int(interval_in_tau),
         param_name %in% names(PARAM_IDX)) %>%
  select(algorithm, sample_index, param_name, value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = param_name, values_from = value)

# maximized logL: ONE row per recording (param columns empty), same cell, same key
logL <- cloud %>% as_tibble() %>%
  filter(component_path == "logL", statistic == "value",
         Num_ch == REP_NCH, group_size == REP_GROUP, near_int(interval_in_tau)) %>%
  select(algorithm, sample_index, logL = value)

# join logL onto the cloud points by the recording key; drop any non-finite logL (failed runs)
d <- pts %>%
  left_join(logL, by = c("algorithm", "sample_index")) %>%
  filter(if_all(c(on, off, unitary_current, Num_ch_mean, logL), is.finite)) %>%
  mutate(algo = factor(ALGO_LAB[algorithm], levels = unname(ALGO_LAB)))

# per-algorithm robust logL scaling (p02..p98 -> 0..1) and Mahalanobis outlier distance from the
# cloud centre, for both pairs (used to test whether low logL == mis-recovered).
robust01 <- function(z) { q <- quantile(z, c(.02, .98), na.rm = TRUE)
  pmin(pmax((z - q[1]) / (q[2] - q[1]), 0), 1) }
maha2 <- function(a, b) { X <- cbind(a, b); mahalanobis(X, colMeans(X), cov(X)) }
d <- d %>% group_by(algo) %>% mutate(
  logL_rel = robust01(logL),
  m_kin    = maha2(on, off),
  m_amp    = maha2(Num_ch_mean, unitary_current)) %>% ungroup()

message(sprintf("rows: %d | algos: %d | non-finite logL dropped during join",
                nrow(d), n_distinct(d$algo)))
```

```{r helpers}
# per-facet Spearman correlation of logL with the outlier (Mahalanobis) distance from the cloud
# centre; ~0 means logL does not track mis-recovery. Placed as a small annotation on each facet.
rho_labels <- function(px, py) {
  mcol <- if (all(c(px, py) %in% c("on","off"))) "m_kin" else "m_amp"
  d %>% group_by(algo) %>%
    summarise(rho = cor(logL, .data[[mcol]], method = "spearman"), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(lab = sprintf("rho == %.2f", rho))
}

# one small-multiple row (5 algorithms) for a parameter pair, points coloured by logL.
# fill_mode = "global"  -> raw logL, single shared viridis, robust GLOBAL clip (faithful to brief)
#           = "peralgo" -> per-algorithm robust-scaled logL (within-cloud structure made visible)
pair_plot_logL <- function(px, py, fill_mode = c("global","peralgo"),
                            show_strip = TRUE, show_rho = FALSE) {
  fill_mode <- match.arg(fill_mode)
  cl <- d %>% transmute(algo, x = .data[[px]], y = .data[[py]], logL, logL_rel) %>%
    filter(is.finite(x), is.finite(y))

  tx <- unname(TRUTH[px]); ty <- unname(TRUTH[py])          # centre every panel on the truth
  hx <- 1.5 * max(abs(quantile(cl$x, c(.01, .99), na.rm = TRUE) - tx))
  hy <- 1.5 * max(abs(quantile(cl$y, c(.01, .99), na.rm = TRUE) - ty))
  truth <- tibble(x = tx, y = ty)

  g <- ggplot(cl, aes(x, y)) +
    { if (fill_mode == "global")
        geom_point(aes(colour = logL), size = .5, alpha = .8)
      else
        geom_point(aes(colour = logL_rel), size = .5, alpha = .8) } +
    geom_point(data = truth, aes(x, y), shape = 3, size = 2.6, stroke = 1,
               colour = "black", inherit.aes = FALSE) +          # the truth cross
    facet_wrap(~ algo, nrow = 1) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = tx + c(-hx, hx), ylim = ty + c(-hy, hy)) +
    labs(x = parse(text = unname(PARAM_LAB[px]))[[1]],
         y = parse(text = unname(PARAM_LAB[py]))[[1]]) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 8) +
    theme(legend.position = "right", panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          strip.text = element_text(size = 8))

  if (fill_mode == "global") {
    clip <- quantile(d$logL, c(.01, .99), na.rm = TRUE)         # robust global clip
    g <- g + scale_colour_viridis_c(name = "logL", limits = clip, oob = squish)
  } else {
    g <- g + scale_colour_viridis_c(name = "logL\n(per-algo\nrescaled\n2-98%)",
                                    limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0, .5, 1),
                                    labels = c("low", "", "high"))
  }
  if (!show_strip) g <- g + theme(strip.text = element_blank(),
                                  strip.background = element_blank())
  if (show_rho) {
    rl <- rho_labels(px, py)
    g <- g + geom_text(data = rl, aes(x = tx - hx * 0.94, y = ty + hy * 0.9, label = lab),
                       parse = TRUE, inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 2.6, hjust = 0, vjust = 1,
                       colour = "grey20")
  }
  g
}
```

```{r plot-peralgo, fig.width = 7.0, fig.height = 4.6}
# The panel that answers the exploratory question: logL rescaled inside each algorithm so within-
# cloud variation shows. Per-facet Spearman rho(logL, outlier distance) is annotated: ~0 everywhere
# means the low-logL (dark) points are NOT the mis-recovered ones -> logL does not flag bad runs.
rowKr <- pair_plot_logL("on", "off", "peralgo", show_rho = TRUE) + labs(tag = "A")
rowAr <- pair_plot_logL("Num_ch_mean", "unitary_current", "peralgo",
                        show_strip = FALSE, show_rho = TRUE) + labs(tag = "B")
g_peralgo <- (rowKr / rowAr) + plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "right", legend.key.height = unit(28, "pt"),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 8), legend.text = element_text(size = 7))
g_peralgo
ggsave("Figure_S2.pdf", g_peralgo, width = 7.0, height = 4.6)
```

```{r diagnostics}
# quantitative version of the annotation: does low logL track mis-recovery?
# rho_kin/rho_amp = Spearman(logL, Mahalanobis outlier distance) for the kinetic / amplitude pair;
# worst5_maha / all_maha = median outlier distance of the worst-5%-by-logL recordings vs all.
d %>% group_by(algo) %>% summarise(
  n            = n(),
  logL_med     = median(logL),
  logL_iqr     = IQR(logL),
  rho_kin      = cor(logL, m_kin, method = "spearman"),
  rho_amp      = cor(logL, m_amp, method = "spearman"),
  worst5_maha  = median(m_kin[logL <= quantile(logL, .05)]),
  all_maha     = median(m_kin),
  .groups = "drop")
```
